rag_rules: Repair AGREE diagnostics by ensuring all identifiers, types, and references are visible in the current AADL scope and match expected AADL property and feature definitions.
When fixing type mismatches, align helper declarations with their expressions using correct AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool).
Do not introduce new package-qualified identifiers or data types that are not present in the analyzed AADL model's configured dependencies.
generic_rules: Validate that all AGREE expressions use only visible AADL features or previously declared helpers within the same annex block.
Ensure type consistency in AGREE constructs: numeric variables must have numeric expressions, boolean variables must have boolean expressions.
Place component type contracts in component type scopes and implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes to maintain architectural correctness.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks with proper syntax for assume, guarantee, eq, and const declarations.
Use only visible identifiers from the AADL model when writing AGREE clauses; avoid referencing undefined or non-existent elements.
anti_patterns: Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers or data types that are not part of the current AADL model’s dependencies.
Do not attempt to fix unresolved references by inventing new declarations; instead, remove or rewrite clauses to use existing visible identifiers.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about missing packages or property sets
Model Analyst Agent tasks related to AADL structural extraction
